mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate-increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 4.5, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur when both series decline, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects real-time economic activity that reinforces short-term market optimism, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO rises, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns falls below 0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 200, rising federal funds rates successfully anchor inflation expectations, reducing macroeconomic uncertainty and supporting positive equity valuations.
failure_condition: Returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 200 and FEDFUNDS rises, or the regime filter shows no predictive alpha compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market fragmentation and weakening productivity, forecasting negative corporate earnings and equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur during payroll-unemployment divergence, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent signals economic contraction that triggers institutional risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market rallies when GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, or the signal fails to predict direction in >50 percent of triggered cases over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight monetary regime, simultaneous weakness in industrial output and auto sales confirms demand destruction, amplifying short-term sell-offs and generating negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur when both supply proxies decline during tightening, or the interaction loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.
